title: TrustForge Conformance Vector
description: A single conformance vector consumed by tf-conformance runners. Vectors describe a category, the inputs the runner needs, and the canonical output (or expected error) every conformant implementation must produce. The format is the wire-level record that backs every category file under conformance/ — schema, signature, chain, framing, session, bridge, relay, trust-overlay, guard, packet, evidence, federation.

vector_version.description: Version of the conformance-vector schema itself.

category.description: Vector category. The runner this vector is dispatched to.

id.description: Stable, human-readable identifier for this vector. Implementations cite this id in their conformance reports.

spec_ref.description: TF-XXXX or DECISIONS.md reference establishing the normative behavior under test.

description.description: Human-readable summary of what this vector exercises.

input.description: Inputs supplied to the runner. Shape varies per category; runners validate the shape they receive.

expected.description: Canonical expected output. Implementations are conformant when their output matches under the canonicalization rule for the category. Implementations may emit any JSON value here.

expect.description: Outcome label when the runner produces a single boolean. Use `expected` for full output equality.

expected_error.description: Expected validation/runtime error when `expect` is `invalid`. Empty object means 'any error'.

fixture.description: Path (relative to repo root) of a YAML/JSON fixture supplying the input.

applies_to_profiles.description: Profile labels this vector belongs to. Empty / omitted means 'every profile'.
